verdicts.note: Evidence confirms an MCP server exists at https://github.com/iPAYX-Technologies/mcp-fx-audit with tools for FX audit
GitHub repo URL is correct for the MCP server
README shows live Cloudflare endpoint (https://wild-bird-a412.ybolduc.workers.dev/mcp) accessible for FX audit tools, with most features working without signup
README confirms 'No signup required' for basic FX audit tools, with optional API key for advanced features
The MCP server uses Bearer token auth (not API key) per README. IXOPAY APIs use Basic Auth, but they appear to be a separate underlying platform, not iPayX's own APIs
The MCP server is not REST - it's an MCP protocol server with 4 tools. IXOPAY's REST APIs appear to be from a different vendor/platform, not iPayX's own API
Should be higher (0.85+) - evidence clearly shows an MCP server exists and is functional
Finance/Fintech category appropriate for FX audit services
Correctly identifies confusion about IXOPAY relationship and the lightweight nature of the MCP wrapper

revised.name: iPayX
revised.one_liner: iPayX provides an FX forensic-audit MCP server (free, mostly no-signup) for detecting hidden bank FX fees. IXOPAY appears to be a separate underlying payments platform referenced in docs.

auth_methods.notes: MCP FX Audit tool: Bearer token via IPAYX_API_KEY env var, optional, required only for live provider quotes/full reports.

revised.self_serve_notes: Core FX audit MCP tools (mid-market rate lookups) work with no signup/API key. Free API key obtainable via iPayX website for live provider comparisons and forensic reports; some advanced reporting is billed per report.

api_surface.notes: This is an MCP server exposing 4 tools (audit_fx_fee, compare_providers, score_invoice, get_mid_market_rate / check_fx_rate) via MCP protocol, not a REST API. IXOPAY platform mentioned in docs appears to be a separate system.

evidence.supports: MCP server exists, free/no-signup basic usage, tools list, live endpoint URL
MCP server package (@ipayx/mcp-fx-audit) exposing 4 FX audit tools
Third-party MCP registry listing confirming existence of iPayX MCP server
IXOPAY Transaction API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Provisioning API uses Basic Auth
IXOPAY Settlement API uses Basic Auth
API key/username setup for Basic Auth credentials
Gateway API requires HMAC signature in Authorization header

flags: IXOPAY appears to be a separate payments platform referenced in documentation, not iPayX's own API
MCP server is a lightweight wrapper around FX rate comparisons, not a full payments API toolkit
Live endpoint URL differs between README (wild-bird-a412.ybolduc.workers.dev) and potentially official endpoint (mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp)
